title: cydonia japonica
intro: Cydonia japonica, also known as the Japanese quince or Japanese medlar, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible fruit. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and polyphenols. The health benefits of Cydonia japonica include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
